# Transcrição: Targeting_Isn't_What_It_Used_to_Be

- **Fonte:** Targeting_Isn't_What_It_Used_to_Be
- **Duração:** 07:23
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. It is time to take a new approach to targeting. Let's go So yeah, let's talk about targeting now this is a topic I've covered in bits and pieces of prior episodes But I really want to take a broader look at targeting generally today I see far too many advertisers who obsess over this who think it's far more important than it is and Who make their results worse in the process and honestly all you're doing is stressing yourself out Unnecessarily you're making it more complicated than it needs to be for the sake of business The reality is this if you're still approaching targeting like it's 2017 We've got a problem now back then targeting was the strategy. I admit it It was central to everything I did There are social media quotes from me still circulating around about how targeting is the most important part of your ad success And it was true then we'd load up on relevant interests and behaviors We'd split test 50 different lookalikes and we felt like a genius when it worked But now that strategy is mostly window dressing So here's the thing if you're optimizing for conversions most of your targeting inputs are treated as suggestions Suggestions not rules so you plug in a lookalike Meta says cool and then expands far beyond it if it thinks it'll help you reach your goal Same with detail targeting so whether you provide detail targeting inputs or not or lookalike audiences Your ads are likely reaching the same people and you know what that means There's rarely a point in segmenting targeting into separate ad sets anymore If the algorithm can reach the same people across ad sets all you're doing is creating overlap Now your budget split up which won't help you exit the learning phase Your ads are competing against one another. It's not strategic. It's wasteful Now let's talk about age and gender. Are you still restricting by age? Because you think your ideal customer is 35 to 44 Are you still excluding men because women convert better? You probably don't need to if you're optimizing for sales The algorithm will figure it out. You've already trained it with conversion data It knows who's buying Restricting age and gender can backfire. It creates limitations

## [03:00 - 06:04]

It adds friction and often it increases costs Now the one place where this can get messy is when you're optimizing for leads When you optimize for leads meta is focused on quantity the cheapest leads possible That's the goal. But if those leads are complete garbage, they're the wrong demo. No intent low quality You might have to step in in that case. So maybe that means restricting by age or gender Maybe it means optimizing for a deeper event, but only make those changes when there's a clear problem Otherwise stay hands-off. So here's the targeting setup that I recommend Start with location. If you're national go broad include the full countries Don't get cute by isolating the states or the regions that you think will give you the best results Now if you're local target those locations Now just know that local business targeting isn't perfect It includes people who live there and people who are just passing through age and gender Leave it wide open unless you're proven otherwise Seriously, unless you've tested and confirmed that it improves performance. Don't touch it Detailed targeting and lookalikes sure Add them as suggestions if you want knock yourself out, but don't expect them to do much They're not what they used to be and please Don't segment by ad set unless it's completely necessary Don't split up interest groups or lookalikes into their own ad sets. There's no point Meta is going to expand anyway and don't run one ad set for men and one for women Unless again, there's a very good reason for doing so Same for age You're just watering down your budget and competing with yourself now if you want better targeting put it in your ad creative That's where it happens Speak directly to the people you want Repel the ones that you don't make sure that the right people Complete the action that you want and that's done with the ad copy creative and offer That's where targeting lives now So this is the bottom of the glass. This is what I want you to walk away with today Detailed targeting and lookalikes don't do much when you're optimizing for conversions Meta treats them as suggestions and fills in the gaps on its own So if you're segmenting audiences splitting up ad sets Narrowing age and gender You're probably not helping you're just giving the system more to work around so if the results aren't broken Stop fixing them. Let the algorithm breathe Let it learn if you want to control who sees your ads Speak to the people in your creative because that's where the real targeting happens now
